title: M2 forecast training
text: deployed v16_fresh values. Use nested LOSO promoting different specification. Production training uses nested leave-one-season-validation. every outer held-season, M0 M1 tuning, feature construction, model selection, calibration must learned without season’s future observations. outer fold used prospective scoring.
code: library(PAGe)  spec <- stage2_make_spec(   delta = 0, Kr = 1, k_f = 4, k_e = 2,   alpha_state = 0.15, k_r = 0, k_de = 0, k_sp = 6,   bias_alpha = 0.05, bias_beta = 0 ) m2 <- train_m2(historical, m0, m1, best_spec = spec)

title: M2 forecast training
text: fitted GAM remains frozen deployment. level-Holt EMA updates forecast bias errors observed far active season, season-specific effect estimated online eligible post-ignition observations. updates must use errors whose targets already observed issue date. Use Bernoulli negative log likelihood primary probabilistic score retain horizon-specific calibration absolute-error summaries. Pooling across weeks can conceal poor performance near ignition seasonal peak.

title: Getting started with PAGe
text: Use mode = "retune" full M0/M1/M2 tuning. adaptive M2 grid informed compatible prior results supports "min_nll", "one_se", "pareto" selection. Optional conservative racing removes clear losers; finalists still receive full nested-LOSO evaluation.

The 2025-26 holdout is excluded by default. training <- train_pipeline(allD, mode = "refresh") kit <- training$kit

title: Getting started with PAGe

title: Pipeline overview
text: deployed v16_fresh M2 specification uses k_f=4, k_e=2, alpha_state=0.15, k_sp=6, k_r=0, k_de=0, delta=0, Kr=1, bias_alpha=0.05, bias_beta=0 (recorded nested-LOSO Bernoulli NLL 0.4175). Deployment freezes GAM updates level-Holt bias correction post-ignition online season effect. Weekly refitting production default.

title: Pipeline overview
text: train_pipeline(mode="refresh") refits locked specification. mode="retune" builds adaptive grid compatible prior results, keeps incumbent diverse finalists, adds neighbors, expands reached boundaries. Minimum NLL default selection rule; one-standard-error Pareto selection explicit alternatives. Optional staged racing eliminates clear losers always evaluates survivors full nested LOSO. 2025-26 season replayed unseen holdout. may enter training 2026-27 promotion demonstrates least 2% NLL improvement, 5% horizon-MAE degradation, 10% phase-MAE degradation.

title: Prospective deployment
text: orchestration performs following operations order: validate normalize current snapshot; run M0 determine current ignition state; run M1 using observations available issue week; inject M1 phase uncertainty covariates M2 snapshot; generate 1- 2-week forecasts apply online correction.
code: library(PAGe)  kit <- load_prospective_kit("m2_production.rds") result <- run_prospective_pipeline(   kit = kit,   current_data = current_snapshot )

title: Prospective deployment
text: Treat trained kit immutable identify version hash. Use explicit manual ignition overrides record reason one. Reject snapshots inconsistent counts, duplicate season-weeks, feature scale differs training. Never update historical forecast revised observations arrive. Store revisions separately original -issued result. Monitor M0 state, M1 peak status spread, raw M2 predictions, corrected predictions; distinguish model drift upstream data failures. Stage-specific entry points (run_m0(), run_m1(), run_m2()) support diagnostics, run_pipeline() preferred high-level interface.

title: Phase-Aligned Gated Epidemic Forecasting
text: PAGe forecasts seasonal respiratory-virus positivity one two weeks ahead. three-stage M0 -> M1 -> M2 pipeline detects epidemic ignition, aligns partial season historical reference curves, predicts frozen binomial GAM plus adaptive bias correction.

title: Phase-Aligned Gated Epidemic Forecasting

title: Phase-Aligned Gated Epidemic Forecasting
text: Surveillance observations bundled. Supply authorized historical CSV explicitly set PAGE_FLU_HIST_FILE, validate canonical data contract.
code: library(PAGe)  allD <- load_flu_hist("/authorized/path/flu_history.csv") |>   prepare_surveillance_data()  # Offline locked-spec refresh; 2025-26 remains a holdout by default. training <- train_pipeline(allD, mode = "refresh") kit <- training$kit

title: Phase-Aligned Gated Epidemic Forecasting
text: default gates require 2% NLL improvement, horizon MAE degradation 5%, phase MAE degradation 10%. failed malformed report keeps holdout excluded.
code: candidate <- replay_season_holdout(kit, allD, season = "2025-26") incumbent <- replay_season_holdout(incumbent_kit, allD, season = "2025-26") promotion <- check_promotion(candidate$metrics, incumbent$metrics)  # A passing report permits 2025-26 to enter the 2026-27 training refresh. next_training <- train_pipeline(allD, mode = "refresh", promotion = promotion)

title: Phase-Aligned Gated Epidemic Forecasting
text: adaptive grid uses compatible prior results, retains v16 incumbent diverse finalists, adds local neighbors, expands reached boundaries. Optional conservative racing requires user-supplied fold evaluator; surviving candidates always receive full nested-LOSO evaluation. deployed v16 reference specification k_f = 4, k_e = 2, alpha_state = 0.15, k_sp = 6, k_r = 0, k_de = 0, delta = 0, Kr = 1, bias_alpha = 0.05, bias_beta = 0 (recorded nested-LOSO Bernoulli NLL 0.4175). See vignette("intro", package = "PAGe"), pipeline overview, walkthrough.
code: retuned <- train_pipeline(   allD,   mode = "retune",   previous_results = prior_m2_results,   selection_method = "min_nll", # or "one_se" / "pareto"   racing = FALSE )

title: Apply Bayesian shrinkage + bias correction to a peak estimate — .apply_peak_calibration

Applies two corrections sequentially: Shrinkage (C): pulls t_peak toward historical     prior mean, weighted ratio prior variance data variance     (CI width). Early-season wide CIs → heavy shrinkage. Bias correction (): subtracts residual bias predicted     GAM fitted LOSO errors function t_since_ign. text: alignedD Data frame least: season, newWeek, y, neg. Methods "gaussian_logit" "median_smooth" also require fit (estimateDerivs()). exSeason Character vector season labels exclude. k Basis dimension cyclic smooth. n_weeks Integer. Number weeks template domain (default 52). nAGQ Passed gamm4; default 1 (Laplace). used binomial methods. method Character. Estimation method: "binomial" Binomial GAMM raw counts via gamm4 (original). "binomial_weighted" Binomial GAMM raw counts ignition--peak     count inflation (like estimateDerivs weighting). "gaussian_logit" Gaussian GAM logit(fit) trough downweighting.     Requires fit column. "median_smooth" Pointwise median per-season fit across seasons     newWeek, smooth cyclic GAM. Requires fit column. "fs" Factor-smooth interaction via s(newWeek, season, bs="fs").     season gets smooth shared smoothness penalty; population     curve = average per-season predictions. Requires fit column. "gaussian_logit_fs" Combined: global cyclic smooth s(newWeek, bs="cc")     plus factor-smooth s(newWeek, season, bs="fs") per-season shape     deviations. Population curve global smooth (fs excluded     prediction). Requires fit column. trough_weight Numeric (0,1]. Weight pre-season (phase==0) rows. used "gaussian_logit" "binomial_weighted". Default 0.1. peak_weight_boost Numeric >= 1. Count inflation factor ignition--peak rows. used "binomial_weighted". Default 3. agg Character. Aggregation method "fs" method's population curve: "median" (default) takes pointwise median across seasons logit scale; "mean" takes mean. Ignored methods.

what: Walk-forward alignment evaluation with LOSO reference curves — loso_walkforward
title: Walk-forward alignment evaluation with LOSO reference curves — loso_walkforward
text: test season (LOSO default): Runs estimateDerivs() + flagIgnition() + alignIgnition()     training seasons build aligned training dataset. Fits reference curve estimateRef() training     aligned data. Runs run_ignition_weekly() prospectively raw test-season     data simulate real-time ignition detection. Walks forward walk_start walk_end: ignition     locks iWeek_hat, re-anchors data     newWeek = weekF - iWeek_hat + anchorWeek produces alignment +     forecast step.

title: Walk-forward alignment evaluation with LOSO reference curves — loso_walkforward
text: allD Raw data frame (one row per season-week) least season, weekF, y, N (neg), p. params Named list Stage-1 detector threshold parameters passed run_ignition_weekly() (e.g. stage1_tuning.rds$best_params). walk_start Integer NULL. First weekF produce forecast. NULL (default) starts ignition-lock week. walk_end Integer NULL. Last weekF evaluate. NULL (default) uses last observed week season. manual_labels Named integer vector mapping season labels verified ignition weekF values, passed flagIgnition() training seasons. NULL forces algorithmic detection. train_seasons Character vector training season labels. NULL (default) uses seasons except test season (LOSO). test_seasons Character vector test season labels. NULL (default) evaluates seasons. exclude_seasons Character vector season labels exclude training testing. Useful known outlier seasons (e.g. "2015-16"). NULL (default) excludes nothing. k_deriv Basis dimension passed estimateDerivs() (default 10). k_ref Basis dimension passed estimateRef() (default 10). n_weeks Integer. Template domain length estimateRef() (default 52). flag_args Named list additional arguments forwarded flagIgnition() (excluding df manual_labels). allow_scale Logical NULL. Passed check_scale_identifiability(). NULL auto-detects per fold. level Numeric. CI level forecast intervals (default 0.95). use_ci Logical. Forwarded run_alignment_prospective(). TRUE (default), peak declared passed last observed newWeek exceeds upper CI bound. buffer_weeks Integer. Forwarded run_alignment_prospective(). Additional weeks beyond threshold declaring peak passed (default 0L). n_cores Integer. Parallel workers eval_weeks loop per season. Defaults parallel::detectCores() - 1. Set 1 disable. min_obs Integer. Minimum observations ignition attempting alignment (default 4). curvature_ratio Numeric coefficient activating dilation. template_shift Integer shift applied template coordinates. peak_weight_boost Numeric >= 1. Multiplicative weight observations ignition peak estimateDerivs() (default 1 = boost). peak_weight_decay Numeric > 0. Exponential decay rate weights observed peak (default 0.3). align_trough_weight, align_rise_weight, align_peak_decay Numeric alignment-loss weights epidemic regions. use_multi_template Logical; use multi-template ensemble. ref_method Reference-curve method passed estimateRef(). multi_temperature, multi_top_k, multi_blend_alpha Ensemble controls. slope_weight, slope_window Growth-rate similarity controls. dynamic_temp, dynamic_temp_pivot Early-season temperature controls. checkpoint_file Character path (NULL). provided, saves incremental results RDS file test season completes. restart, completed seasons loaded checkpoint skipped. Delete file force full rerun. verbose Logical. Print per-season progress (default TRUE).

text: Orchestrates five steps single held-season: build fold -> M1 train -> M2 train -> M1 test -> M2 eval. Returns aggregated results; handles errors gracefully.

title: Run a complete nested LOSO fold — nested_loso_run_fold
text: allD Full multi-season data frame. test_season Character scalar - held-season. params M0 detection parameters. spec M2 hyperparameter spec object. exclude_seasons Character vector seasons exclude entirely. horizons Integer vector forecast horizons (default c(1L, 2L)). eval_window Integer; max weeks post-ignition (default 12L). k_deriv Integer; basis dim derivatives (default 10L). k_ref Integer; basis dim reference curve (default 10L). n_weeks Integer; reference curve period (default 52L). ref_method Reference-curve method passed estimateRef(). manual_labels Optional manual ignition labels. flag_args Named list forwarded flagIgnition(). allow_scale Passed M1 walk-forward. use_ci Logical; peak CI control (default TRUE). buffer_weeks Integer; peak buffer (default 0L). min_obs Integer; minimum rows alignment (default 4L). curvature_ratio Numeric; delta curvature gate (default 1.0). temperature, rise_weight, trough_weight, peak_decay Ensemble alignment-loss controls. slope_weight, slope_window Growth-rate similarity controls. dynamic_temp, dynamic_temp_pivot Early-season temperature controls. top_k, blend_alpha Template filtering blending controls. method GAM fitting method (default "REML"). parallel Logical; parallelize M1 walk-forward (default TRUE). skip_m1 Logical; reuse supplied M1 predictions supported. verbose Logical; print progress.

title: Run a complete nested LOSO fold — nested_loso_run_fold
text: named list: scores One-row tibble metrics. predictions Tibble per-observation predictions. m1_preds M1 test-season predictions tibble. fold fold object nested_loso_build_fold().

text: Production-ready function called per week updated season data. Performs real-time ignition detection, alignment, forecast, peak passage detection, returning current state season.

code: run_alignment_prospective(   currentSeason,   ref,   hyper,   params = NULL,   ign_out = NULL,   use_ci = TRUE,   buffer_weeks = 0L,   allow_scale = NULL,   level = 0.95,   min_obs = 4L,   cal = NULL,   curvature_ratio = 1,   time_weights = NULL,   trough_weight = 0.1,   rise_weight = 1,   peak_decay = 0.3 )

title: Prospective alignment and peak detection for one season — run_alignment_prospective
text: currentSeason Data frame ongoing season current weekF. Must columns weekF, y, either N neg. ref Output estimateRef() (pre-computed training data). hyper Output learn_alignment_hyperparams() (pre-computed training data). params Named list Stage-1 detector threshold parameters. used ign_out NULL. One params ign_out must supplied. ign_out Pre-computed output run_ignition_weekly(). NULL (default), runs ignition detection internally using params. use_ci Logical; TRUE (default), peak declared passed last observed newWeek exceeds upper CI bound peak. FALSE, uses point estimate . buffer_weeks Integer. Additional weeks beyond peak threshold required declaring peak passed (default 0L). allow_scale Logical NULL. NULL (default), scale identifiability determined automatically via check_scale_identifiability(). level Numeric. Confidence level prediction intervals (default 0.95). min_obs Integer. Minimum number rows currentSeason required attempting alignment (default 4L). cal Optional peak-calibration object. curvature_ratio Numeric coefficient activating dilation. time_weights Optional observation weights. trough_weight, rise_weight, peak_decay Alignment-loss controls.

title: Prospective alignment and peak detection for one season — run_alignment_prospective
text: named list components: state Character: "pre_ignition" (ignition yet locked),     "aligning" (ignition locked, actively forecasting),     "post_peak" (peak detected, alignment can stop). iWeek_hat Integer. Estimated ignition week original weekF     space (NA pre-ignition). ign_week_locked Integer. First weekF ignition     confirmed (NA pre-ignition). tau Numeric. Time-shift alignment parameter. delta Numeric. Scale (dilation) alignment parameter. Numeric. Lower asymptote. b Numeric. Upper asymptote. allow_scale Logical. Whether scale fitting enabled. delta_on Logical. Whether dilation active. t_peak Numeric. Estimated peak newWeek space. t_peak_ci Numeric length-2. 95% CI peak newWeek     space. peak_weekF Integer. Estimated peak original weekF space. peak_weekF_lo Integer. Lower CI bound peak weekF     space. peak_weekF_hi Integer. Upper CI bound peak weekF     space. peak_passed Logical. TRUE peak considered     passed. fallback_reason Character NA. Reason partial     fallback alignment, . forecast_df Tibble. Prediction data frame     align_forecast_pipeline_dilate() (NULL pre-ignition). ign_out List. Full output run_ignition_weekly().

title: Prospective alignment and peak detection for one season — run_alignment_prospective
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ref    <- readRDS("data/ref.rds") hyper  <- readRDS("data/hyper.rds") params <- readRDS("data/stage1_tuning.rds")$best_params  # Called once per week as new data arrives ap <- run_alignment_prospective(   currentSeason = current_data,   ref           = ref,   hyper         = hyper,   params        = params ) ap$state       # "pre_ignition", "aligning", or "post_peak" ap$peak_weekF  # estimated peak in original week space  # Pass previous ign_out to avoid re-running ignition each week ap2 <- run_alignment_prospective(   currentSeason = current_data_next_week,   ref           = ref,   hyper         = hyper,   params        = NULL,   ign_out       = ap$ign_out ) } # }

what: Score an ignition-threshold grid on historical seasons — score_params
title: Score an ignition-threshold grid on historical seasons — score_params
text: Calibrates (-sample) ignition thresholds scoring row parameter grid historical ignition weeks (`iWeek`) across seasons `alignedD_prosp`.

title: Score an ignition-threshold grid on historical seasons — score_params
code: score_params(   grid,   alignedD_prosp,   models_all,   penalty = 99,   parallel = TRUE,   ncores = max(1L, parallel::detectCores() - 1L),   chunked = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Score an ignition-threshold grid on historical seasons — score_params
text: grid `data.frame` containing one row per parameter setting. Must contain columns: `cls_thr`, `p0_thr`, `p_thr`, `d1_thr`, `p_thr2`, `d1_thr2` (passed [detectIgnition4()] list). alignedD_prosp Historical dataset containing seasons. Must include columns `season` `iWeek`, plus whatever [detectIgnition4()] needs (e.g., `weekF`, `p`, `y`, `N`, `d1_link`, `d2_link`). models_all Output [fitIgnitionModels4()] fit full historical dataset. penalty Numeric penalty added ignition detected season. Default `99`. parallel Logical; `TRUE`, use PSOCK parallelism score rows `grid`. Default `TRUE`. ncores Integer number worker processes use `parallel=TRUE`. Default `detectCores()-1`. chunked Logical; `TRUE`, split work `ncores` chunks reduce per-task overhead. Default `TRUE`. verbose Logical; print progress messages. Default `TRUE`.

title: Score an ignition-threshold grid on historical seasons — score_params
text: numeric vector length `nrow(grid)` giving mean loss   row `grid`, order `grid`.

title: Score an ignition-threshold grid on historical seasons — score_params
text: parameter row, function loops seasons calls [detectIgnition4()] get estimated ignition week. loss : $$\frac{1}{S}\sum_{s=1}^S \left[ (\hat{iWeek}_s=\mathrm{NA})\cdot \mathrm{penalty} + (1-)\cdot |\hat{iWeek}_s - iWeek_s| \right]$$ `penalty` applied ignition detected. Parallel execution Windows-safe using PSOCK cluster via `parallel`.

title: Score an ignition-threshold grid on historical seasons — score_params
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # 1) Fit models once on all historical data models_all <- fitIgnitionModels4(alignedD_prosp, verbose = TRUE)  # 2) Build a parameter grid to calibrate thresholds grid <- expand.grid(   cls_thr = seq(0.60, 0.85, by = 0.05),   p0_thr  = seq(0.010, 0.040, by = 0.010),   p_thr   = seq(0.010, 0.030, by = 0.005),   d1_thr  = seq(0.001, 0.005, by = 0.001),   p_thr2  = seq(0.005, 0.020, by = 0.005),   d1_thr2 = seq(0.0005, 0.0030, by = 0.0005),   KEEP.OUT.ATTRS = FALSE,   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  # 3) Score the grid (parallel on Windows-safe PSOCK) grid$mean_loss <- score_params(   grid = grid,   alignedD_prosp = alignedD_prosp,   models_all = models_all,   penalty = 99,   parallel = TRUE,   ncores = 4,   chunked = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )  # 4) Pick best parameters best <- grid[which.min(grid$mean_loss), ] params_hat <- as.list(best) } # }

text: dat Multi-season data.frame required columns. grid data.frame parameter combinations. missing parameter columns among `cls_thr`, `p_cum_thr`, `p_thr`, `prev_thr`, `n_consec`, `N`, `w_min`, `w_max` filled defaults (see ). miss_penalty Numeric. Penalty added per missing season detection (`iWeek_hat=NA`). Default 20. lambda Numeric. Weight worst-case absolute error `max_abs` combined `score`. Default 10. sum_tol Numeric >= 0. Tolerance applied forming candidate set minimizing `sum_abs`: keep rows `sum_abs <= min_sum + sum_tol`. Default 0. ncores Integer >= 1. Number cores. 1, runs serially. title: Grid search ignition detection parameters (OS-aware parallel) — tuneIgnitionGrid
text: list : best_params Named list best parameter values (subset columns `grid`). results data.frame = `grid` plus evaluation metrics (`score`, `sum_abs`, `max_abs`, `n_miss`, `mean_abs`, `sd_abs`). best_row Single-row data.frame containing best parameter set metrics.

title: Grid search ignition detection parameters (OS-aware parallel) — tuneIgnitionGrid
text: evaluation parallelized OS-aware way: - Windows: PSOCK cluster (`parallel::makeCluster()` + `parLapply()`) - Linux/macOS: forked processes (`parallel::mclapply()`) ## Required columns `dat` must contain: - `season`, `weekF`, `phase`, `p`, `p_cls_p`, `y`, `N` ## Truth definition season, "true" ignition week : `iWeek_true = min(weekF[phase == 1])`. ## Scoring parameter set, function computes: - `diff = iWeek_hat - iWeek_true` - `sum_abs = sum(abs(diff))` across seasons (ignoring `NA` diffs) - `max_abs = max(abs(diff))` across seasons (worst-case; `Inf` missing) - `n_miss =` number seasons `iWeek_hat = NA` - `score = sum_abs + lambda * max_abs + miss_penalty * n_miss` Selection lexicographic: 1) minimize `sum_abs` 2) among parameter sets `sum_abs <= min(sum_abs) + sum_tol`, minimize `max_abs` 3) tie-breakers: minimize `n_miss`, minimize `score`

title: PAGe 0.2.0
text: Introduces coherent public workflow surveillance-data validation, training, holdout replay, promotion, frozen-kit forecasting, result summaries. Makes frozen-GAM deployment default keeps weekly refitting available explicit compatibility option. Adds refresh full-retune training modes prior-informed adaptive M2 grids, minimum-NLL, one-standard-error, Pareto selection, plus optional conservative candidate racing. Treats 2025-26 external holdout default requires explicit, threshold-based promotion may enter next training cycle. Removes private data-path assumptions. Historical surveillance data must supplied explicitly PAGE_FLU_HIST_FILE.
